# 7-Day Meal Plan
### For a 31-Year-Old Australian Adult
**Allergies/Intolerances:** Shellfish, Gluten, Lactose, Onion
**Preferences:** Meat, Potatoes, Mexican, Thai, Indian flavours
**Sensitivity:** Super-taster — mild, adjustable seasoning throughout
**All prices approximate AUD, budget-focused**

---

> **A few quick notes before we start:**
> - "Adjustable" means seasoning is added at the table or kept minimal in cooking — you control the intensity
> - Coconut milk used throughout is **full-fat canned** (naturally lactose-free)
> - All soy sauce used is **tamari** (gluten-free soy sauce)
> - Corn tortillas are used instead of flour tortillas (naturally gluten-free — check the label to confirm no wheat starch added)
> - Garlic is used in place of onion for allium flavour where desired, but can be omitted or reduced if you find it similarly problematic
> - "GF" = confirmed gluten-free product — always check labels, as manufacturing processes vary

---

## DAY 1 — Monday

### 🍳 Breakfast — Scrambled Eggs with Potato Hash
- 2 eggs scrambled in olive oil
- 1 medium potato, diced small and pan-fried until golden
- Fresh tomato slices on the side
- Salt and pepper to taste at the table

**Prep note:** Dice potato the night before and store in cold water to save morning time. Pat dry before frying.

---

### 🥗 Lunch — Simple Chicken and Rice Bowl
- 1 grilled chicken thigh (seasoned with a pinch of cumin and paprika, or plain)
- ½ cup steamed jasmine rice
- Sliced cucumber and carrot
- A drizzle of olive oil and a squeeze of lemon

**Prep note:** Cook extra chicken at dinner the night before and refrigerate for easy lunch assembly.

---

### 🍽️ Dinner — Mild Beef Tacos (Mexican)
- 150g lean beef mince, cooked with a small pinch of cumin, smoked paprika, and garlic (optional)
- 3 small corn tortillas (GF-confirmed)
- Toppings: diced tomato, shredded lettuce, sliced avocado
- Squeeze of lime

**Prep note:** Keep spices on the side so you can season your own portion to taste. Cook mince simply — the toppings carry the flavour.

---

### 🍎 Snacks
- A banana
- A small handful of plain rice crackers (GF)
- A few carrot sticks

---
---

## DAY 2 — Tuesday

### 🍳 Breakfast — Banana Oat Smoothie Bowl
- ½ cup certified **gluten-free rolled oats**, soaked overnight in water or coconut milk
- 1 banana, sliced on top
- A drizzle of maple syrup (very small amount, optional)
- A few pumpkin seeds

**Prep note:** Soak oats in the fridge overnight — no cooking needed in the morning.

> ⚠️ **Important:** Regular oats are often cross-contaminated with wheat. Buy oats labelled **certified gluten-free** (brands like Coles Finest GF Oats or Bob's Red Mill GF Oats are available in Australia).

---

### 🥗 Lunch — Thai-Style Chicken Salad
- 1 cooked chicken breast, shredded (leftover or freshly poached)
- Mixed salad greens, shredded carrot, cucumber, fresh coriander (if tolerated)
- Dressing: 1 tbsp tamari, 1 tsp sesame oil, squeeze of lime, tiny pinch of chilli flakes (optional)

**Prep note:** Dress lightly — the dressing is strong, so use less than you think you need and add more at the table.

---

### 🍽️ Dinner — Mild Thai Green Curry with Chicken and Potato
- 150g chicken thigh, cut into chunks
- 1 medium potato, cubed
- 1 cup canned coconut milk
- 1–2 tsp **GF Thai green curry paste** (check label — some contain shrimp paste or gluten; Mae Ploy or Maesri brands are widely available and many are GF, but verify)
- A handful of frozen peas or green beans
- Served over jasmine rice

**Prep note:** Start with just 1 teaspoon of curry paste and taste before adding more. The coconut milk mellows the heat significantly.

---

### 🍎 Snacks
- A small handful of unsalted mixed nuts
- Apple slices
- Rice crackers (GF) with a thin spread of avocado

---
---

## DAY 3 — Wednesday

### 🍳 Breakfast — Fried Egg and Sweet Potato
- 2 eggs, fried in olive oil (sunny side up or over easy)
- ½ a sweet potato, sliced into rounds and pan-fried or roasted
- Sliced tomato

**Prep note:** Roast sweet potato rounds the night before at 200°C for 20 minutes — reheat in a dry pan in the morning.

---

### 🥗 Lunch — Leftover Thai Curry (from Tuesday dinner)
- Reheat last night's curry
- Serve over fresh jasmine rice or with plain rice crackers on the side

**Prep note:** Always make extra dinner portions — it saves money and time.

---

### 🍽️ Dinner — Mild Indian Butter Chicken (Dairy-Free Version)
- 150g chicken thigh, cut into pieces
- Sauce: ½ can coconut milk, 2 tbsp tomato paste, ½ tsp each of cumin, coriander, mild paprika, a tiny pinch of garam masala (optional), 1 small garlic clove (optional)
- Served over jasmine rice
- Fresh coriander to garnish (optional)

**Prep note:** This is a simplified, mild version. Coconut milk replaces cream perfectly here. Add spices gradually and taste as you go — you can always add more but can't take it away.

---

### 🍎 Snacks
- A boiled egg
- A banana
- Plain corn chips (GF — check label) with fresh salsa (diced tomato, lime, a pinch of salt)

---
---

## DAY 4 — Thursday

### 🍳 Breakfast — Corn Tortilla Breakfast Wrap
- 2 scrambled eggs
- 2 small corn tortillas (GF)
- Sliced avocado, diced tomato
- Optional: a tiny pinch of smoked paprika on the eggs

**Prep note:** Warm tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds each side — makes them pliable and much tastier.

---

### 🥗 Lunch — Simple Tuna and Potato Salad
- 1 can of tuna in olive oil (drained) — **not** in brine (lower sodium)
- 2 medium potatoes, boiled and cooled, cut into chunks
- Sliced cucumber, cherry tomatoes
- Dressing: olive oil, lemon juice, a pinch of pepper

**Prep note:** Boil potatoes the night before and refrigerate. This lunch takes under 5 minutes to assemble.

---

### 🍽️ Dinner — Indian-Spiced Lamb Mince with Rice
- 150g lamb mince
- Spices: ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp coriander, ¼ tsp turmeric, pinch of garam masala (all adjustable)
- 1 can diced tomatoes (no added salt variety if possible)
- A handful of frozen peas stirred in at the end
- Served over jasmine rice

**Prep note:** This is essentially a simplified keema. Cook mince first, drain excess fat, then add spices and tomatoes. Simmer 15 minutes.

---

### 🍎 Snacks
- Carrot and cucumber sticks
- A small handful of pumpkin seeds
- A piece of fruit (apple or orange)

---
---

## DAY 5 — Friday

### 🍳 Breakfast — GF Oat Porridge with Banana
- ½ cup certified GF rolled oats cooked in water or coconut milk
- 1 banana sliced on top
- A tiny drizzle of honey (optional — very small amount)

**Prep note:** Cook oats in water for a less rich result, or coconut milk for a creamier, naturally sweet porridge that needs no added sweetener.

---

### 🥗 Lunch — Mexican Rice Bowl
- Leftover lamb mince (from Thursday) or freshly cooked beef mince
- ½ cup cooked rice
- Diced tomato, sliced avocado, shredded lettuce
- Squeeze of lime

**Prep note:** This is a great "clean out the fridge" lunch. Use whatever vegetables you have left from the week.

---

### 🍽️ Dinner — Thai-Style Beef Stir-Fry with Rice Noodles
- 150g beef strips (or thinly sliced chicken)
- 1 nest of **rice noodles** (GF — check label)
- Vegetables: broccoli, carrot, capsicum (whatever is on hand)
- Sauce: 1 tbsp tamari, 1 tsp sesame oil, squeeze of lime, tiny pinch of chilli (optional), 1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water to thicken

**Prep note:** High heat, quick cooking — have everything prepped and ready before the pan goes on. Don't overcrowd the pan or the meat will steam instead of fry.

---

### 🍎 Snacks
- A boiled egg
- Rice crackers (GF) with avocado
- A handful of grapes or an orange

---
---

## DAY 6 — Saturday

### 🍳 Breakfast — Big Weekend Breakfast
- 2 eggs (any style)
- 2 rashers of bacon (check label — most plain bacon is GF, avoid flavoured varieties)
- Pan-fried potato slices
- Grilled tomato halves
- No toast — the potato fills that role

**Prep note:** A proper weekend breakfast. Take your time. Season at the table.

---

### 🥗 Lunch — Chicken and Avocado Rice Paper Rolls
- **Rice paper wrappers** (GF — naturally gluten-free)
- Filling: cooked chicken breast (shredded), sliced avocado, cucumber, carrot, fresh mint or coriander (optional)
- Dipping sauce: tamari with a squeeze of lime (keep it light)

**Prep note:** Dip rice paper in warm water for 10–15 seconds until just pliable — don't over-soak or they tear. Roll tightly. These are fun to make and very fresh-tasting.

---

### 🍽️ Dinner — Mild Indian Potato and Chickpea Curry (Chana Aloo)
- 1 can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
- 2 medium potatoes, cubed and boiled until just tender
- 1 can diced tomatoes
- Spices: ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp coriander, ¼ tsp turmeric, pinch of garam masala
- 1 garlic clove (optional)
- Served with jasmine rice

**Prep note:** A budget-friendly, filling, meat-free dinner. Chickpeas are very cheap and high in protein. Simmer everything together for 20 minutes until the sauce thickens.

---

### 🍎 Snacks
- Plain corn chips (GF) with guacamole (mashed avocado, lime, pinch of salt)
- A banana
- A small handful of unsalted nuts

---
---

## DAY 7 — Sunday

### 🍳 Breakfast — Coconut Chia Pudding
- 3 tbsp chia seeds
- 1 cup coconut milk
- Stir together the night before and refrigerate
- Top with sliced banana or fresh strawberries in the morning

**Prep note:** This takes 2 minutes to prepare the night before. Stir once after 10 minutes to prevent clumping, then leave overnight.

---

### 🥗 Lunch — Simple Roast Chicken and Potato Soup
- Leftover roast chicken (see dinner below — cook extra) or 1 chicken breast
- 2 potatoes, diced
- 2 cups GF chicken stock (check label — Massel brand is a good GF option in Australia)
- A pinch of cumin or just salt and pepper
- Simmer 20 minutes, roughly mash some potato for thickness

**Prep note:** A gentle, comforting soup. Very mild. Season at the table.

---

### 🍽️ Dinner — Simple Roast Chicken with Roasted Potatoes and Vegetables
- 4–6 chicken thighs (bone-in, skin-on — cheapest and most flavourful)
- 3–4 medium potatoes, cut into wedges
- Carrot and capsicum, roughly chopped
- Drizzle of olive oil, pinch of smoked paprika, salt and pepper (all adjustable)
- Roast at 200°C for 40–45 minutes

**Prep note:** This is the easiest dinner of the week. Everything goes in one tray. Make extra chicken for Monday's lunch to start the cycle again. Rest the chicken 5 minutes before serving.

---

### 🍎 Snacks
- Apple slices
- A boiled egg
- Rice crackers (GF) with avocado

---
---

# 📋 General Prep Notes & Tips

---

### 🔪 Batch Cooking Strategy
- **Cook extra rice** every time — refrigerate and reheat throughout the week. Rice keeps 3–4 days in the fridge.
- **Cook extra chicken** at dinner — use it for the next day's lunch. This is the single biggest time and money saver.
- **Boil a batch of potatoes** every 2–3 days — they reheat quickly in a pan and form the base of many meals.
- **Hard-boil 4 eggs** at the start of the week — keep in the fridge for quick snacks.

---

### 🌶️ Super-Taster Tips
- **Always start with half** the suggested spice amount and taste before adding more
- **Add spices at the table** where possible — chilli flakes, paprika, and cumin can all be sprinkled on after cooking
- **Coconut milk is your friend** — it naturally mellows and rounds out spice flavours
- **Lime and lemon juice** add brightness without heat or saltiness — use freely
- **Avoid pre-made spice blends** where possible — they often contain hidden salt and stronger flavours than expected. Individual spices give you full control.

---

### 🏷️ Label-Reading Reminders for Australia
- **Tamari:** Look for "gluten-free" on the label — Kikkoman GF Tamari is widely available at Coles and Woolworths
- **Corn tortillas:** Some brands add wheat starch — Mission brand corn tortillas are generally GF but always verify the current label
- **Oats:** Must say **certified gluten-free** — standard oats are cross-contaminated
- **Curry paste:** Mae Ploy and Maesri are popular and many varieties are GF, but check each flavour individually as formulations vary
- **Chicken stock:** Massel brand is GF and widely available — regular stock cubes often contain gluten
- **Bacon:** Plain middle bacon is usually GF — avoid honey, maple, or flavoured varieties which may contain gluten or excess sugar
- **Rice crackers and corn chips:** Most plain varieties are GF but check for "may contain wheat" warnings if you are highly sensitive

---

### 🧊 Food Safety Notes
- Cooked rice should be cooled quickly, stored in the fridge within 1 hour, and consumed within 3 days
- Cooked chicken keeps 3–4 days refrigerated
- Avocado: buy firm and ripen on the bench — once ripe, refrigerate and use within 2 days
- Chia pudding keeps 3 days in the fridge — make a double batch on Sunday night

---

*This plan is designed to be flexible — swap proteins between days freely, and adjust seasoning to your comfort. The goal is simple, affordable, genuinely enjoyable food that works with your body, not against it.*

# 7-Day Meal Plan
**Tailored for:** Gluten-free, shellfish-free, lactose-free, onion-free, super-taster friendly (mild, adjustable seasoning)

**Key principles:**
- Onion swapped with leek greens, garlic, chives, or asafoetida (gluten-free brand) where flavour is needed
- Lactose-free milk, hard aged cheeses (naturally low-lactose), or coconut/lactose-free yoghurt
- Naturally gluten-free grains: rice, corn (certified GF), potatoes
- Seasonings served on the side so you can adjust to taste

---

## DAY 1
- **Breakfast:** Scrambled eggs with rice, fresh chives, side of avocado
- **Lunch:** Mexican-style rice bowl — rice, seasoned beef mince (cumin + paprika), corn, lettuce, lime
- **Dinner:** Roast chicken thighs with baked potatoes and steamed carrots
- **Snacks:** Banana; rice crackers with peanut butter

## DAY 2
- **Breakfast:** Lactose-free yoghurt with banana and a few crushed peanuts
- **Lunch:** Leftover roast chicken + potato into a warm potato-chicken salad (olive oil, lemon, chives)
- **Dinner:** Thai-style beef stir-fry — beef strips, capsicum, carrot, garlic, ginger, tamari (GF soy), over rice
- **Snacks:** Carrot sticks; corn chips (certified GF)

## DAY 3
- **Breakfast:** Potato hash with eggs and garlic
- **Lunch:** Tacos (corn tortillas) with beef mince, lettuce, tomato, lime
- **Dinner:** Mild Indian chicken curry — chicken, tomato, garlic, ginger, mild curry powder, coconut milk, served with rice
- **Snacks:** Apple; handful of plain salted peanuts (light salt)

## DAY 4
- **Breakfast:** Lactose-free milk smoothie — banana, peanut butter, lactose-free milk
- **Lunch:** Leftover chicken curry with rice
- **Dinner:** Mexican stuffed potatoes — baked potato, beef mince, corn, grated aged cheddar, coriander
- **Snacks:** Rice crackers; cucumber slices

## DAY 5
- **Breakfast:** Omelette with grated aged cheddar and chives
- **Lunch:** Rice bowl with leftover beef, corn, capsicum, lime
- **Dinner:** Thai-style chicken & vegetable rice — chicken, carrot, capsicum, coconut milk, ginger, garlic
- **Snacks:** Banana; corn chips

## DAY 6
- **Breakfast:** Fried eggs with hash potatoes
- **Lunch:** Leftover Thai chicken rice
- **Dinner:** Beef and potato mild Indian curry — beef chunks, potato, tomato, mild spices, rice on side
- **Snacks:** Apple; peanuts

## DAY 7
- **Breakfast:** Lactose-free yoghurt with banana
- **Lunch:** Loaded potato — baked potato, leftover beef curry, aged cheddar
- **Dinner:** Simple grilled chicken with mashed potato (lactose-free milk + olive oil) and steamed carrots
- **Snacks:** Carrot/cucumber sticks; rice crackers with peanut butter

---

## GROCERY LIST

**Proteins**
- Chicken thighs (1.5 kg)
- Beef mince (1 kg)
- Beef strips/chunks (700 g)
- Eggs (18)

**Produce**
- Potatoes (3 kg)
- Carrots (1 kg)
- Capsicum (4)
- Lettuce (1)
- Tomatoes (6)
- Cucumber (2)
- Avocado (2)
- Bananas (8)
- Apples (4)
- Lemons (2), limes (4)
- Garlic, fresh ginger
- Chives, coriander (fresh herbs)
- Leek (use green tops)

**Pantry / Dairy**
- Rice (2 kg)
- Corn tortillas (certified GF)
- Corn chips (certified GF)
- Rice crackers
- Tinned corn (2)
- Coconut milk (3 tins)
- Lactose-free milk (2 L)
- Lactose-free yoghurt (1 large tub)
- Aged cheddar (naturally low lactose)
- Peanut butter; plain peanuts
- Olive oil
- Tamari (GF soy sauce)

**Spices (mild, adjustable)**
- Cumin, paprika, mild curry powder, black pepper, salt (use sparingly)

---

## PREP NOTES

- **Batch-cook rice** every 2–3 days; refrigerate and reheat (cool quickly, store ≤3 days).
- **Roast extra chicken/potatoes** on Day 1 and Day 3 curries to cover next-day lunches.
- **Onion replacement:** sauté garlic + finely sliced leek greens in oil as your aromatic base; a tiny pinch of GF asafoetida adds onion-like depth.
- **Super-taster tip:** cook curries and stir-fries mild, then offer extra spice, lime, pepper, or fresh herbs at the table so each portion can be adjusted.
- **Salt control:** season at the end and taste as you go; rinse tinned corn to reduce sodium.
- **Cross-contamination:** check labels on tamari, corn products, and spice blends for "gluten free" and "may contain shellfish" warnings.
- **Cheese note:** aged hard cheeses are naturally very low in lactose; skip if sensitive and use extra avocado/olive oil instead.

Would you like a version with estimated costs in AUD or a vegetarian day swapped in?

Here’s a 7-day, budget-minded, shellfish-free, gluten-free, lactose-free, onion-free meal plan built around meat, potatoes and Mexican/Thai/Indian flavors. Spices and salt are kept low—adjust to taste.

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
Day 1  
Breakfast  
• 2 scrambled eggs with handful baby spinach and chopped tomato  
• 1 slice gluten-free toast (low-salt)  

Lunch  
• Mexican chicken bowl:  
 – Grilled chicken strips (season lightly with garlic powder, cumin, paprika)  
 – ½ cup cooked white rice  
 – ¼ cup canned black beans (rinsed)  
 – Corn kernels, chopped tomato, chopped coriander, lime wedge  

Dinner  
• Thai chicken-veg stir-fry:  
 – Sliced chicken breast, red capsicum, carrot sticks, snow peas  
 – Sauce: 1 tbsp mild peanut butter, splash coconut milk, garlic, grated ginger, lime juice, water to thin  
 – Serve with ½ cup jasmine rice  

Snacks (choose any two)  
• Apple slices + 1 tbsp almond butter  
• Carrot sticks  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
Day 2  
Breakfast  
• Porridge: ½ cup gluten-free oats + almond milk, topped with banana slices and cinnamon  

Lunch  
• Beef & sweet-potato bowl:  
 – Pan-fried lean beef mince with garlic, ground cumin, coriander, turmeric  
 – Roasted cubed sweet potato  
 – Baby spinach, chopped tomato, drizzle olive oil + lemon  

Dinner  
• Indian-style chicken curry:  
 – Chicken pieces simmered with garlic, ginger, turmeric, cumin, coriander, canned tomato, coconut milk  
 – ½ cup basmati rice  

Snacks  
• Rice cakes + peanut butter  
• Cucumber slices  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
Day 3  
Breakfast  
• Smoothie: almond milk + banana + handful spinach + 1 tbsp chia seeds  

Lunch  
• Quinoa & roast-potato salad:  
 – Cooked quinoa + roasted diced potato + capsicum + cucumber + cooked chicken  
 – Dressing: olive oil, lemon, minced garlic, chopped parsley  

Dinner  
• Mexican beef tacos:  
 – Gluten-free corn tortillas  
 – Lean beef mince seasoned with garlic powder, cumin, paprika  
 – Shredded lettuce, tomato salsa (tomato, coriander, lime), avocado slices  

Snacks  
• Handful mixed nuts  
• Orange  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
Day 4  
Breakfast  
• Coconut yogurt + gluten-free granola + mixed berries  

Lunch  
• Thai beef salad:  
 – Grilled beef strips  
 – Mixed greens, carrot ribbons, cucumber, coriander  
 – Dressing: lime juice, fish sauce (low-salt), garlic, a touch of sugar/honey, water  

Dinner  
• Potato & chickpea curry:  
 – Canned chickpeas + cubed potato cooked with garlic, ginger, turmeric, cumin, coriander, canned tomato, water or broth  
 – Serve with ½ cup rice  

Snacks  
• Rice crackers + guacamole (avocado + lime + garlic)  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
Day 5  
Breakfast  
• Egg “muffins”: whisked eggs + chopped spinach + diced capsicum, baked in muffin tin  

Lunch  
• Chicken & potato salad:  
 – Leftover roasted potato + shredded chicken + baby spinach  
 – Dressing: olive oil, lemon, garlic, chopped parsley  

Dinner  
• Mexican stuffed capsicum:  
 – Halved capsicums filled with mixture of beef mince (garlic, cumin, paprika), canned black beans, tomato, bake until tender  

Snacks  
• Apple + small handful almonds  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
Day 6  
Breakfast  
• Sweet-potato hash: diced sweet potato sautéed in oil + garlic, topped with fried egg  

Lunch  
• Leftover curry (Day 2 or Day 4) with rice or a green salad  

Dinner  
• Thai coconut chicken soup (Tom Kha-style):  
 – Chicken pieces + sliced mushroom + carrot + garlic + ginger + lemongrass (or zest of lime) + coconut milk + chicken broth  
 – Finish with lime juice, coriander  

Snacks  
• Banana + 1 tbsp peanut butter  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––  
Day 7  
Breakfast  
• Chia pudding: chia seeds soaked overnight in almond milk + vanilla extract, topped with berries  

Lunch  
• Beef & veg stir-fry:  
 – Sliced beef + broccoli, carrot, capsicum, garlic, ginger, low-salt tamari (gluten-free soy sauce)  
 – Serve with roasted potato cubes  

Dinner  
• Indian spiced lentil soup (dal):  
 – Red lentils + garlic, ginger, turmeric, cumin, coriander, canned tomato, water or broth  
 – Garnish coriander, serve with rice  

Snacks  
• Carrot sticks + tahini dip (tahini + lemon + garlic + water)  

––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––––

Below is a cheap, simple 7‑day meal plan that avoids shellfish, gluten and lactose and omits onion. It leans on meat, potatoes and Mexican/Thai/Indian flavours while staying mild and easily adjustable for a super‑taster (seasonings suggested on the side). Each day includes breakfast, lunch, dinner and simple snacks. After the plan is a single grocery list (approx. quantities for one adult for 7 days) and brief prep notes.

Important notes before you start
- Always choose certified gluten‑free oats, corn tortillas and tamari/coconut aminos if you buy them. Read labels for “contains wheat” or malt.
- Use lactose‑free or plant milks/yoghurt (almond, oat, soy, coconut) and dairy‑free margarine if needed.
- No onion is used; replace the savoury depth with garlic (small amounts), cumin, coriander, turmeric, lemon, fresh herbs and a pinch of asafoetida only if it is made without wheat (most commercial asafoetida mixes contain wheat so avoid unless labelled gluten‑free).
- Keep salt, chilli flakes and hot sauces on the side so you and any guests can season to taste.
- If you avoid all fish products, skip fish sauce in Thai recipes and use extra salt or tamari/coconut aminos instead.

7‑day meal plan (mild, adjustable)

Day 1
- Breakfast: GF oats porridge made with almond milk, mashed banana and cinnamon.  
- Lunch: Roast potato cubes + grilled chicken thigh on salad greens (cucumber, tomato, spinach) with lemon + olive oil dressing.  
- Dinner: Mexican beef mince tacos: beef mince gently cooked with cumin, smoked paprika, garlic powder and canned tomato; serve on corn tortillas with shredded lettuce, tomato, avocado and lime wedges. (Serve chilli flakes on side.)  
- Snacks: apple; small handful of unsalted nuts.

Day 2
- Breakfast: Scrambled eggs with baby spinach; 1 corn tortilla or rice on side.  
- Lunch: Leftover tacos beef in a rice bowl: brown/white rice, diced tomato, avocado, fresh coriander, squeeze of lime.  
- Dinner: Mild Thai coconut chicken curry: chicken pieces, potato chunks, carrot, canned coconut milk, ginger, turmeric and a little tamari or fish sauce (optional). Serve with rice. (Serve extra lime and sliced green chilli on side.)  
- Snacks: carrot sticks + hummus (no onion); banana.

Day 3
- Breakfast: 2 boiled eggs and a small baked potato with mashed avocado and cracked pepper.  
- Lunch: Chickpea salad: canned chickpeas (rinsed), cucumber, tomato, coriander, lemon juice, cumin, olive oil. Serve with lettuce leaves or rice.  
- Dinner: Baked herb chicken thighs (rosemary or mixed herbs) with roasted potatoes and steamed green beans.  
- Snacks: rice cakes (GF) with peanut butter; orange.

Day 4
- Breakfast: Overnight GF oats with chia, almond milk and frozen berries.  
- Lunch: Red lentil & potato soup (no onion): red lentils, diced potato, carrot, canned tomato, turmeric, cumin — simmer until soft. Serve with GF bread or rice.  
- Dinner: Mexican potato & corn frittata: eggs, leftover roast potato (small cubes), sweetcorn, capsicum, mild paprika; serve with salad and salsa (tomato + lime + coriander).  
- Snacks: coconut yoghurt (unsweetened) with a few nuts; apple.

Day 5
- Breakfast: Omelette with tomato and spinach, fresh herbs; corn tortilla or GF toast.  
- Lunch: Leftover Thai chicken curry over rice or with potato wedges.  
- Dinner: Indian‑inspired beef keema (use beef mince): mince cooked with canned tomato, peas, turmeric, cumin, coriander powder, garam masala (salt & chilli mild). Serve with rice or boiled potato.  
- Snacks: cucumber sticks + hummus; pear.

Day 6
- Breakfast: Banana + oat pancakes (blend GF oats, 1 banana, 1 egg) — pan fry in a little oil; top with berries.  
- Lunch: Tuna & potato salad: canned tuna (in springwater), boiled potato, chopped cucumber, parsley, lemon, pepper.  
- Dinner: Mild Thai beef stir‑fry: thin beef strips, mixed frozen veg, ginger, a splash of tamari or coconut aminos, served with rice. (Serve chilli or extra tamari separately.)  
- Snacks: popcorn (lightly salted) ; boiled egg.

Day 7
- Breakfast: Porridge or leftover overnight oats; add a spoon of nut butter if desired.  
- Lunch: Chicken & mixed veg tray bake (leftovers reheated) or chicken rice bowl: leftover roast chicken, steamed green veg, carrot, rice and lemon.  
- Dinner: Indian aloo chana (potato & chickpea curry): potato + chickpeas in tomato base, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala; serve with rice.  
- Snacks: carrots or celery sticks; small handful of unsalted nuts.

Simple swaps & tips
- If you prefer more meat days, increase chicken or beef portions and reduce legumes.  
- If fish sauce is not wanted in Thai dishes, use tamari/coconut aminos + a little sugar substitute (small) or extra coconut milk for body.  
- Keep chilli flakes, tamari, lemon/lime and fresh herbs on the table so you can add flavour slowly — ideal for super‑tasters.

Brief prep notes (batching & time savers)
- Batch cook rice: cook 3–4 cups dry rice at once, store in fridge for several meals. Reheat with splash of water.  
- Roast a large tray of potatoes and some chicken thighs at once (seasoned simply) early in the week; use leftovers for lunches, frittata or salads.  
- Make a big pot of lentil/potato soup and/or chickpea curry midweek — these reheat well for lunches and dinners.  
- Cook beef mince in one go: divide into portions, one for tacos, one for keema, one for rice bowls. Add different seasonings to vary the flavour.  
- Keep dressings and strong condiments separate. Serve lemon/lime wedges, chilli flakes and tamari on the side so individuals add little at a time.  
- For the Thai curries: use 1 can coconut milk + 1 cup stock/water for a lighter sauce. Add fish sauce/tamari to taste — small amounts first.  
- For Mexican flavours: use cumin, paprika and a pinch of smoked paprika for depth without needing chilli heat. Add fresh coriander and lime at serving time.  
- Use leftover soft boiled eggs and boiled potatoes for quick salads and snacks.

Seasoning strategy for a super‑taster
- Start very mild: small pinch of salt when cooking, minimal spice in the pan. Always offer extra seasoning at the table.  
- Use fresh herbs, lemon or lime juice to brighten flavour without adding salt.  
- Offer a tray of condiments (lime, tamari, chilli flakes, fresh coriander) so each mouthful can be adjusted.

### **Important Notes Before You Start**

*   **"Gluten-Free" in Australia:** Always check labels. In Australia, products labelled "Gluten-Free" must comply with the Food Standards Code, meaning no detectable gluten. Be cautious with oats; only use those specifically labelled gluten-free due to cross-contamination.
*   **Lactose-Free:** This plan uses lactose-free dairy alternatives. Always check labels, as some products (like some margarines) may contain lactose or milk solids.
*   **Alliums (Onion/Garlic):** This is the biggest challenge for flavour. We will rely on asafoetida (hing) – a resin used in Indian cooking that, when cooked, provides an onion/garlic flavour. It is potent, so start with a pinch. We'll also use the green tops of spring onions (shallots) for a fresh, mild flavour, as the intolerance is usually to the bulb.
*   **Super-Taster & Seasoning:** Salt is kept low. Spices are added in two stages: a mild base during cooking, with extra served on the side (e.g., chilli flakes, fresh herbs) so you can adjust to your preference.
*   **Budget Tips:** Buy generic/home brand canned tomatoes, legumes, and spices. Use frozen veggies where possible (e.g., corn, spinach). A whole chicken is cheaper than pre-cut portions.

### **Brief Prep Notes**

*   **Sunday (Prep Day):**
    1.  Cook a large batch of jasmine and basmati rice for the week.
    2.  Wash and chop vegetables (carrots, celery, potatoes) for the stew and roast. Store in water in the fridge.
    3.  Make the week's snacks: portion nuts, make hummus (blend chickpeas, tahini, lemon juice, salt).
    4.  Roast the chicken and vegetables for Sunday dinner.

*   **Cooking Without Alliums:**
    *   **Asafoetida (Hing):** Use a **tiny pinch** (about 1/8 tsp) fried in oil at the start of making a curry or stew to build a savoury base flavour.
    *   **Spring Onion Greens:** Finely chop the green tops and add at the very end of cooking for a fresh, mild flavour.
    *   **Optional Garlic:** If your intolerance allows for *very small* amounts of garlic infused oil (where the solids are removed), this can be another option. **Introduce with extreme caution.**

*   **Adjustable Seasoning:** Always serve meals with lemon/lime wedges, fresh herbs (coriander), and a small dish of chilli flakes on the side. This allows you to add bright and spicy flavours to your own taste after serving.

*   **Leftovers:** Portion out lunches immediately after dinner and store in the fridge. Most dinners will keep for 3-4 days.

## **Prep Notes**

- **Overnight oats:** Mix oats, milk, chia, and fruit in jars; refrigerate overnight.
- **Chicken and potato salad:** Boil potatoes and chicken, chop veg, toss with olive oil and lemon.
- **Beef and potato curry:** Sauté beef, garlic, ginger, add diced potato, carrot, mild curry powder, canned tomatoes, simmer.
- **Tuna/potato patties:** Mix mashed potato, tuna, egg, parsley, GF crumbs; shape and pan-fry.
- **Thai beef stir-fry:** Sauté beef, add veg, garlic, ginger, splash of tamari; serve over rice.
- **Potato and lentil curry:** Sauté garlic, ginger, add diced potato, carrot, lentils, curry powder, tomatoes, simmer.
- **Mexican skillet:** Brown beef, add potato, capsicum, spices, tomatoes; simmer, top with coriander and serve with chips.
- **Chicken and rice soup:** Simmer chicken, carrot, celery, rice, parsley, season mildly.
- **Thai coconut curry:** Sauté chicken, ginger, add potato, carrot, coconut milk, simmer, finish with coriander.
- **Turkey patties:** Mix turkey mince, grated veg, egg, GF crumbs; form patties, pan-fry.
- **Indian stew:** Brown beef, add potato, carrot, ginger, spices, tomatoes, simmer.

**Snacks** are mostly grab-and-go or require minimal prep.

---

### **Tips**
- Adjust all spice/seasoning to taste; start mild and add more if desired.
- All curries and stews freeze well; make extra for future meals.
- Use lemon juice, fresh herbs, and mild spices for flavor without overwhelming super-taster palates.
- All recipes are onion-free, gluten-free, lactose-free, and shellfish-free.

Below is a sample 7‐day meal plan that is both budget‐friendly and simple to prepare. It has been designed for a 31‐year‐old Australian adult with shellfish and gluten allergies as well as lactose and onion intolerance. The recipes focus on meat, potatoes, and bold yet mild flavors inspired by Mexican, Thai, and Indian cuisine. Adjust salt and spices with care (using garlic, ginger, herbs, and mild spices) to suit a super‐taster’s palate. All dairy is substituted with lactose‐free or plant alternatives, and all gluten‐containing ingredients and onions have been omitted.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 1
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast:
• Gluten‐free oats made with almond milk, topped with sliced banana.
Lunch:
• Grilled chicken salad with lettuce, sliced tomato, cucumber, and avocado. Drizzle a little olive oil and lemon juice (or your preferred mild dressing).
Dinner:
• Baked herb potatoes (cube potatoes tossed in olive oil, rosemary, and a pinch of salt) served with a grilled steak and a side of roasted bell peppers.
Snacks:
• Carrot and cucumber sticks with a simple, homemade hummus (blend chickpeas, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, and tahini—omit any onion).
• An apple, sliced.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 2
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast:
• Rice cakes spread with mashed avocado and a dash of pepper.
Lunch:
• Mexican-style chicken bowl: Shredded (roasted or boiled) chicken on a bed of cooked rice with black beans, corn, diced tomato, chopped avocado, fresh coriander, and a squeeze of lime.
Dinner:
• Thai-inspired basil chicken: Sauté ground chicken with garlic and a small amount of mild red chili (optional) and fresh basil; serve over jasmine rice.
Snacks:
• Gluten-free crackers with a dairy-free cheese spread.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 3
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast:
• Scrambled eggs (use dairy‐free butter or olive oil) with fresh spinach. Serve with gluten-free toast.
Lunch:
• Beef and potato stew: Slowly simmer beef cubes with potatoes, carrots, celery, diced tomato, garlic, and mild dried herbs (skip onions) until tender.
Dinner:
• Indian-inspired chicken curry: Use chicken pieces simmered in a sauce made with coconut milk, garlic, ginger, and a mild blend of cumin, coriander, and turmeric. Serve with rice.
Snacks:
• Sliced apple or pear with a spoonful of peanut butter (ensure no added salt/sugar).

──────────────────────────────
DAY 4
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast:
• Gluten-free cereal with almond milk and a handful of mixed berries.
Lunch:
• Chicken and vegetable stir-fry: Sauté diced chicken with bell peppers, snap peas, carrots, and garlic. Serve over rice.
Dinner:
• Baked potatoes loaded with seasoned ground beef, diced tomato, and corn; top with a dairy-free cheese alternative.
Snacks:
• A small handful of unsalted mixed nuts and a few dried fruits.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 5
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast:
• Smoothie made with banana, almond milk, a handful of spinach, and a scoop of gluten‐free protein powder (optional).
Lunch:
• Thai peanut chicken salad: Combine grilled chicken strips with lettuce, shredded carrots, and sliced cucumber. Toss with a mild peanut dressing (blend peanut butter, a little water, lime juice, and a pinch of garlic powder—omit onion).
• Serve with a side of rice noodles (made from rice).
Dinner:
• Indian spiced lamb patties: Season lamb mince lightly with garlic, ginger, and mild garam masala. Form into patties and pan‐cook. Serve with boiled potatoes and steamed green beans.
Snacks:
• A couple of rice cakes topped with almond butter.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 6
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast:
• Gluten-free toast with scrambled eggs and a side of sliced tomato.
Lunch:
• Mexican beef lettuce wraps: Cook ground beef with diced tomato, bell peppers, corn, and mild spices (cumin, a pinch of chili powder). Spoon the mixture into crisp lettuce leaves and top with avocado slices.
Dinner:
• Thai green curry with chicken: Gently simmer chicken pieces in a sauce made from coconut milk and a mild green curry paste (check that it contains no onion). Serve with jasmine rice.
Snacks:
• Sliced cucumber and bell peppers with a mild hummus dip.

──────────────────────────────
DAY 7
──────────────────────────────
Breakfast:
• Buckwheat pancakes (buckwheat is naturally gluten‐free) made with a bit of almond milk. Serve with fresh fruit and a light drizzle of pure maple syrup.
Lunch:
• Chicken and potato soup: Lightly boil chicken pieces with diced potatoes, carrots, celery, garlic, and herbs (use a homemade chicken stock or one verified to be free of onion) until the vegetables are tender.
Dinner:
• Pan‐seared steak with roasted potatoes and a medley of mixed vegetables (such as zucchini, bell peppers, and mushrooms) tossed in olive oil and mild herbs.
Snacks:
• A banana or apple with a small handful of unsalted nuts.

Prep & Cooking Notes
──────────────────────────────
• Batch-cook items like rice, quinoa, or boiled potatoes early in the week to save time.
• Prepare larger portions of soups, stews, and curries so that leftovers can be enjoyed the next day.
• For homemade hummus, blend chickpeas with garlic, lemon juice, tahini, and olive oil. Adjust texture with a little water if needed.
• When seasoning, start with a light sprinkle of herbs and spices and taste as you go to accommodate sensitivity.
• Many of these dishes (curries, stews, and grilled meats) allow you to adjust spice levels during or after cooking—offer extra lemon/lime wedges or dairy-free yogurt on the side to cool or brighten flavors.
• Always check packaged ingredients (such as curry paste, canned beans, or stock) to ensure they are free of gluten, lactose, and onion.

Here is a cheap, simple, and customizable 7-day meal plan tailored for an Australian adult. 

To accommodate your super-taster sensitivity, this plan uses mild, sweet-leaning vegetables (like carrots and corn) and avoids bitter greens. All spices are listed in small amounts so you can adjust them to your palate. To ensure no hidden onion, gluten, or shellfish, we are using simple DIY spice blends instead of pre-made sauces or pastes.

*Note for Australian Gluten Allergies: In Australia, oats cannot legally be classified as gluten-free. Therefore, this plan uses Rice Flakes for a warm, porridge-style breakfast.*

### **The 7-Day Meal Plan**
*Strategy: To save money and time, every dinner makes 2 portions. You will eat one portion for dinner, and pack the leftovers for lunch the next day.*

**Day 1**
*   **Breakfast:** Warm Rice Flake Porridge (1/2 cup rice flakes cooked in 1 cup Lactose-Free milk) topped with half a sliced banana and a pinch of cinnamon.
*   **Lunch:** Quick Potato & Egg Hash (Pan-fry 1 diced, pre-microwaved potato with 2 eggs and a pinch of mild paprika).
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Mexican Beef & Potato Hash** (Cooks 2 portions). Brown 250g beef mince with 2 diced potatoes, 1/2 diced red capsicum, and 1/2 cup frozen corn. Season with 1/4 tsp cumin, 1/4 tsp mild paprika, and a pinch of oregano. 
*   **Snack:** Plain rice crackers with peanut butter.

**Day 2**
*   **Breakfast:** 2 Fried eggs on 1 pan-fried diced potato.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Mexican Beef & Potato Hash.
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Thai Coconut Chicken & Rice** (Cooks 2 portions). Sauté 250g diced chicken breast, 1/2 sliced zucchini, and 1 sliced carrot. Add 1/2 cup canned coconut milk, 1/2 tsp freshly grated ginger, and a squeeze of fresh lime. Serve over cooked white rice.
*   **Snack:** 1 Apple, sliced.

**Day 3**
*   **Breakfast:** Warm Rice Flake Porridge with banana and cinnamon.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Thai Coconut Chicken & Rice.
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Indian Beef & Potato Curry** (Cooks 2 portions). Brown 250g beef mince and 2 diced potatoes. Add 1/2 cup frozen peas, 1/2 cup coconut milk, 1/4 tsp turmeric, 1/4 tsp ground coriander, and 1/4 tsp cumin. Simmer until potatoes are soft.
*   **Snack:** Carrot sticks with peanut butter.

**Day 4**
*   **Breakfast:** 2 Fried eggs on 1 pan-fried diced potato.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Indian Beef & Potato Curry.
*   **Dinner:** **Mexican Chicken & Corn Tacos** (Cooks 2 portions). Sauté 250g diced chicken, 1/2 diced red capsicum, and 1/2 cup frozen corn with 1/4 tsp cumin. Serve in 4-6 warm Gluten-Free corn tortillas with a squeeze of lime.
*   **Snack:** Plain rice crackers and an apple.

**Day 5**
*   **Breakfast:** Warm Rice Flake Porridge with banana and cinnamon.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Mexican Chicken Tacos.
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Thai Chicken & Zucchini Stir-fry** (Cooks 2 portions). Sauté 250g diced chicken, 1/2 sliced zucchini, and 1 sliced carrot. Season with 1/2 tsp fresh ginger, a squeeze of lime, and a tiny pinch of salt. Serve over white rice.
*   **Snack:** Plain rice crackers with peanut butter.

**Day 6**
*   **Breakfast:** 2 Fried eggs on 1 pan-fried diced potato.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Thai Chicken Stir-fry.
*   **Dinner:** **Mild Indian Potato & Egg Curry** (Cooks 2 portions - a great cheap meal!). Boil 4 eggs. In a pan, simmer 2 diced potatoes and 1/2 cup frozen peas in 1/2 cup coconut milk, 1/4 tsp turmeric, 1/4 tsp coriander, and 1/4 tsp cumin. Halve the boiled eggs and gently stir them in. Serve with rice.
*   **Snack:** 1 Apple, sliced.

**Day 7**
*   **Breakfast:** Warm Rice Flake Porridge with banana and cinnamon.
*   **Lunch:** Leftover Indian Potato & Egg Curry.
*   **Dinner:** **Mexican Beef & Potato "Nachos"** (Cooks 2 portions). Slice 2 potatoes into thin rounds, toss in oil, and bake/pan-fry until crispy. Top with 250g browned beef mince, leftover capsicum, and leftover corn, seasoned with 1/4 tsp cumin. 
*   **Snack:** Carrot sticks and rice crackers.

---

### **Grocery List**
*(Designed to be budget-friendly at Coles, Woolworths, or Aldi)*

**Produce:**
*   1.5kg bag washed white potatoes (cheap and versatile)
*   1 bunch bananas
*   4 apples
*   1 large red capsicum
*   2 medium zucchinis
*   1 bag carrots
*   1 small knob fresh ginger
*   2 fresh limes

**Meat & Dairy (Fridge):**
*   750g Beef mince (regular or lean)
*   750g Chicken breast or thigh fillets
*   1 dozen eggs
*   1L Lactose-Free milk

**Pantry:**
*   1 box Rice Flakes (found in the cereal or health food aisle - safe GF alternative to oats)
*   1 bag White rice (Jasmine or Basmati)
*   1 packet Gluten-Free Corn Tortillas (check ingredients to ensure no wheat/onion)
*   1 jar Peanut Butter (100% peanuts, no added salt/sugar)
*   1 packet plain Rice Crackers
*   *Check you have:* Cooking oil (olive or canola), salt, pepper, ground cumin, ground coriander, ground turmeric, mild paprika, dried oregano, cinnamon.

**Canned & Frozen:**
*   2 cans (400ml each) full-fat or lite coconut milk (check ingredients: just coconut and water)
*   1 bag frozen corn kernels
*   1 bag frozen peas

---

### **Brief Prep & Cooking Notes**
1.  **Super-Taster Adjustments:** The spice measurements (1/4 tsp) are deliberately very low. Taste your food right before serving. If you want more flavor, add another pinch. If it tastes bitter or overwhelming, add a tiny squeeze of lime juice or a splash more coconut milk to mellow it out.
2.  **Potato Hack:** To save time on the breakfast hashes and dinner curries, prick your whole potatoes with a fork and microwave them for 3-4 minutes until slightly soft *before* dicing and pan-frying. They will crisp up in the pan in a fraction of the time.
3.  **Coconut Milk Storage:** You will open one can of coconut milk on Day 2, and finish it on Day 3. You will open the second can on Day 6. Store opened coconut milk in a sealed container in the fridge.
4.  **Salt & Sugar:** This plan contains zero added sugar and relies on you adding a tiny pinch of salt to your own preference, keeping it well within your low-salt/low-sugar requirements.
